task_completion.visual_evidence: The GeoServer web interface (http://localhost:8080/geoserver/web/) is visible in the web browser.
The 'Styles' page is open, typically accessed from the left-hand navigation menu under the 'Data' section.
In the 'New Style' page, the Name text field contains exactly 'blue_polygon'.
The Format dropdown or selection is set to 'SLD' (Styled Layer Descriptor) version 1.0.0.

task_completion.description: Define the fill and stroke colors in the SLD editor
task_completion.visual_evidence: The style editor text area contains XML/SLD code with the fill color set to '#0000FF', stroke color set to '#000080', and stroke width set to '1' (e.g., <CssParameter name="fill">#0000FF</CssParameter>).
A success message is displayed, or the browser returns to the Styles list where 'blue_polygon' is now visible in the table of styles.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoServer web GUI to create the style
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the GeoServer web interface in a browser, rather than using terminal commands (like curl) to interact with the REST API.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GeoServer style editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The SLD code is seen inside the GeoServer web page's text editor area, not just in a local text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: The agent clicked the save/submit button
integrity.visual_evidence: There is evidence of the form being submitted (e.g., clicking 'Submit', 'Save', or 'Validate', followed by a page load or success indicator), rather than just typing the code and stopping.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected style name is 'blue_polygon'. The SLD must contain a fill color of '#0000FF' (pure blue) and a stroke color of '#000080' (dark blue/navy). The format must be SLD 1.0.0. Look for these exact hexadecimal values in the style editor.
